description_wiki: Hamburg, officially the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg, is the second-largest city in Germany after Berlin and seventh-largest city in the European Union, with a population of over 1.9 million.

description_wiki: The University of Washington (UW, and informally U-Dub or U Dub) is a public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States.

description: U.S. civilian mariners
description_wiki: The United States Merchant Marine refers collectively to U.S.-flagged commercial vessels, both privately and federally owned, and the civilian mariners who crew them.

description: New York City Private Independent School
description_wiki: The Ethical Culture Fieldston School (ECFS), also known more simply as Fieldston or Ethical Culture, is a private pre-K through twelfth grade coeducational school in New York City with two campuses, in Manhattan and in the Bronx.

description: college in Yellow Springs, Ohio
description_wiki: Antioch College is a private liberal arts college in Yellow Springs, Ohio, United States.

description: public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.

description: metropolitan area in California, United States
description_wiki: The San Francisco Bay Area, commonly known as the Bay Area, is a region of California surrounding and including San Francisco Bay, and anchored by the cities of Oakland, San Francisco, and San Jose.

description: county in California, United States
description_wiki: Marin County (  mə-RIN) is a county located in the northwestern part of the San Francisco Bay Area of the U.S. state of California.

aliases: The bridge that couldn't be built
description: suspension bridge on the San Francisco Bay
description_wiki: The Golden Gate Bridge is a suspension bridge spanning the Golden Gate, the one-mile-wide (1.6 km) strait connecting San Francisco Bay and the Pacific Ocean in California, United States.

description: independent school in Corte Madera, California, USA
description_wiki: Marin Country Day School is an independent coeducational day school serving grades K-8 located in Corte Madera, California.

description: Social and psychological factors that cause alcoholism in families
British actor and comedian (1925–1980)
description_wiki: Peter Sellers (born Richard Henry Sellers; 8 September 1925 – 24 July 1980) was an English actor and comedian.

description: British actor (1914–2000)
description_wiki: Sir Alec Guinness (born Alec Guinness de Cuffe; 2 April 1914 – 5 August 2000) was an English actor.

description: group of three closely-tied contiguous cities in the U.S. state of Washington
description_wiki: The Tri-Cities are three closely linked cities (Kennewick, Pasco, and Richland) at the confluence of the Yakima, Snake, and Columbia Rivers in the U.S. state of Washington.

description: economic and sociopolitical worldview
description_wiki: Marxism is a political philosophy and method of socioeconomic analysis that uses a dialectical materialist interpretation of historical development, known as historical materialism, to understand class relations and social conflict.

description: founding leader of the Soviet Union (1870–1924)
description_wiki: Vladimir Ilyich Ulyanov (22 April [O.S. 10 April] 1870 – 21 January 1924), better known as Vladimir Lenin, was a Russian revolutionary, politician and political theorist.

description: phenomenon in the United States of making accusations of subversion or treason without proper regard for evidence, named after Senator Joseph McCarthy
description_wiki: McCarthyism is a political practice defined by the political repression and persecution of left-wing individuals and a campaign spreading fear of communist and Soviet influence on American institutions and of Soviet espionage in the United States during the late 1940s through the 1950s, heavily associated with the Second Red Scare, also known as the McCarthy era.

description: United States Senator from Vermont
description_wiki: Bernard  Sanders (born September 8, 1941) is an American politician and activist serving as the senior United States senator from Vermont, a seat he has held since 2007.

description: economic programs of U.S. president Franklin D. Roosevelt
description_wiki: The New Deal was a 1933–1938 series of economic, social, and political reforms in response to the Great Depression in the United States under President Franklin D. Roosevelt.

description: 1956 revolution in Hungary
description_wiki: The Hungarian Revolution of 1956 (23 October – 4 November 1956; Hungarian: 1956-os forradalom), also known as the Hungarian Uprising, was an attempted countrywide revolution against the government of the Hungarian People's Republic (1949–1989) and the policies caused by the government's subordination to the Soviet Union (USSR).

description: Regent of Hungary from 1920 to 1944 (1868-1957)
description_wiki: Miklós Horthy de Nagybánya (18 June 1868 – 9 February 1957) was a Hungarian admiral and statesman who was the regent of the Kingdom of Hungary during the interwar period and most of World War II, from 1 March 1920 to 15 October 1944.

description: holistic system of soft tissue manipulation and movement education
description_wiki: Rolfing () is a form of alternative medicine originally developed by Ida Rolf (1896–1979) as Structural Integration.

description: city in Yavapai and Coconino counties in Arizona, United States
description_wiki: Sedona ( si-DOH-nə) is a city that straddles the county line between Coconino and Yavapai counties in the northern Verde Valley region of the U.S. state of Arizona.

aliases: The Chancellor, Masters, and Scholars of the University of Cambridge
description: collegiate public research university in Cambridge, England, United Kingdom
description_wiki: The University of Cambridge is a collegiate public research university in Cambridge, England.

description: private university located in San Rafael, California
description_wiki: Dominican University of California is a private university in San Rafael, California, United States.

description: private university in Cambridge, Massachusetts
description_wiki: Harvard University is a private Ivy League research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States.

description: American statesman and lawyer (1893-1971)
description_wiki: Dean Gooderham Acheson ( ATCH-iss-ən; April 11, 1893 – October 12, 1971) was an American politician and lawyer.

the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
description: island country in north-west Europe
description_wiki: The United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, commonly known as the United Kingdom (UK) or Britain, is a country in northwestern Europe, off the coast of the continental mainland.

description: city and county seat of Lafayette County, Mississippi, United States
description_wiki: Oxford is the 14th most populous city in Mississippi, United States, and the county seat of Lafayette County, 75 miles (121 km) southeast of Memphis.

description: October 1962 confrontation between the Soviet Union, Cuba and the United States
description_wiki: The Cuban Missile Crisis, also known as the October Crisis in Cuba (Spanish: Crisis de Octubre), or the Caribbean Crisis (Russian: Карибский кризис, romanized: Karibskiy krizis), was a 13-day confrontation between the governments of the United States and the Soviet Union, when American deployments of nuclear missiles in the United Kingdom, Italy and Turkey were matched by Soviet deployments of nuclear missiles in Cuba.

description: intergovernmental military alliance between 32 member states
1946-1992 United States Air Force major command; predecessor of Air Force Global Strike Command
description_wiki: Strategic Air Command (SAC) was a Cold War-era United States Department of Defense Specified Command and a United States Air Force (USAF) Major Command (MAJCOM) responsible for command and control of the strategic bomber and intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) components of the United States military's strategic nuclear forces from 1946 to 1992, active for most of the Cold War.

description: British surreal comedy group
description_wiki: Monty Python, also known as the Pythons, were a British comedy troupe formed in 1969 consisting of Graham Chapman, John Cleese, Terry Gilliam, Eric Idle, Terry Jones and Michael Palin.

description: capital and largest city of Denmark
description_wiki: Copenhagen (Danish: København [kʰøpm̩ˈhɑwˀn] ) is the capital and most populous city of both the country of Denmark and the wider Kingdom of Denmark, with a population of 667,000 people in the municipality and 1.4 million in the urban area.

description: sea between Europe, Africa and Asia
description_wiki: The Mediterranean Sea ( MED-ih-tə-RAY-nee-ən) is an intercontinental sea situated between Europe, Asia, and Africa.

description: country in southern Europe
description_wiki: Italy, officially the Italian Republic, is a country in Southern and Western Europe.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.
